reg-027.title_en: Edinburgh bans OOH advertising for fossil fuels AND arms products — unique across the EU (2024)
reg-027.zusammenfassung: In May 2024, the City of Edinburgh Council resolved to no longer permit advertising for fossil fuels and arms products on all municipal advertising spaces. Edinburgh thereby became the first city to explicitly ban both categories simultaneously from public advertising space.
reg-027.kernbefund: The Edinburgh ban applies to all municipal advertising contracts and council-owned spaces. The city references its climate commitments and the Edinburgh Climate Pledge. The ban covers airlines, oil companies, and arms manufacturers, and affects contracts with JCDecaux and Clear Channel, which operate municipal advertising spaces in Edinburgh.
reg-027.relevanz: The combination of fossil fuel and military advertising bans sets a new European standard. Edinburgh demonstrates that municipalities can directly regulate the outdoor advertising industry through the contractual design of their own concessions — without having to wait for national legislation.

reg-028.title_en: Stockholm Region: Court ruling upholds ban on fossil fuel and gambling advertising on public spaces (2024)
reg-028.zusammenfassung: In 2023, the Stockholm Region resolved to no longer permit advertising for fossil products and gambling on all regional advertising spaces (metro, buses, government buildings). In 2024, the Swedish Administrative Court confirmed the legality of the ban following a lawsuit brought by the outdoor advertising industry.
reg-028.kernbefund: The Administrative Court ruled that public bodies have the right to define categories of advertising incompatible with their ethical and ecological commitments. The ruling creates precedent for Scandinavian municipalities. All advertising contracts of the Stockholm Region are affected.
reg-028.relevanz: The Stockholm ruling strengthens the right of municipal and regional authorities across Europe to ban advertising categories on their own spaces. Since public transport advertising is one of the most lucrative OOH segments, the ruling represents a direct economic threat to Clear Channel and JCDecaux, which hold Stockholm contracts.

reg-029.title_en: Florence 2026 and Genoa 2025: First Italian cities to ban fossil fuel OOH advertising
reg-029.zusammenfassung: Genoa resolved in 2025 and Florence in 2026 to ban advertising for fossil fuels and high-emission products (long-haul flights, internal combustion engines) on municipal advertising spaces. Both cities are pioneers in Southern Europe and are setting a trend for other Italian municipalities.
reg-029.kernbefund: The bans follow the model of Amsterdam, The Hague, and Edinburgh and extend to direct advertising for fossil energy providers, short-haul flights, cruises, and new cars with internal combustion engines. Florence explicitly justifies the ban on the grounds of protecting the UNESCO World Heritage cityscape from commercial overload, as well as climate targets.
reg-029.relevanz: The spread of fossil fuel advertising bans to Southern Europe shows that the model is not a Northern European niche. With Florence and Genoa, strategically significant precedents are being established for further Mediterranean metropolises. The loss of these booking volumes directly affects the OOH industry.

reg-030.title_en: UK ASA: Lloyds Bank greenwashing ad banned on OOH screens — December 2024
reg-030.zusammenfassung: In December 2024, the UK Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) banned an outdoor advertising campaign by Lloyds Bank that positioned itself as climate-friendly, even though the bank is one of the largest financiers of fossil fuels in Great Britain. This is one of the first cases in which a financial institution has been explicitly condemned for greenwashing in OOH media.
reg-030.kernbefund: The ASA found that the advertisements created a misleading impression of Lloyds' climate efforts. The bank had placed outdoor screens with CO2-neutrality messaging while its lending portfolio, according to analysis by the NGO ShareAction, has billions invested in oil and gas companies. The ASA ruling ordered the immediate withdrawal of all OOH materials.
reg-030.relevanz: Greenwashing in OOH campaigns is increasingly being penalised by independent regulators. This ruling shows that industries outside the direct fossil fuel sector can also be confronted with outdoor advertising bans when their advertising message contradicts their actual environmental impact.

reg-031.title_en: UK Competition and Markets Authority: Fines of up to 10% of annual turnover for greenwashing advertising from April 2025
reg-031.zusammenfassung: With the entry into force of the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act, the UK competition authority CMA received from April 2025 the power to impose direct fines of up to 10% of global annual turnover on companies making misleading environmental claims in advertising campaigns — including OOH advertising. Previously, the CMA could only bring court proceedings.
reg-031.kernbefund: The CMA's Green Claims Code prohibits vague statements such as "sustainable", "eco-friendly", or "carbon-neutral" without scientifically verifiable evidence. The new enforcement powers allow direct sanctions without litigation. For large OOH operators and their advertising clients, this means that any greenwashing campaign can now directly lead to billion-pound fines.
reg-031.relevanz: OOH spaces are a preferred medium for greenwashing campaigns — large-format images with green imagery and sustainability promises. The CMA powers affect both advertisers (turnover penalty) and potentially OOH network operators if they participate in the dissemination of misleading content.
